div.head: The Acting Secretary of State to the Minister in Iran (Dreyfus)
div.p: 257. Your 289, September 14. From conversations with Iranian Minister and Saleh, Department gathers that Prime Minister is now thinking in terms of an international figure, for example, someone like former Vice President Dawes, whose prestige would bolster position of Iranian Government both at home and abroad. Minister indicates this man need not be necessarily a specialist in finance.

If this is the case, and if you see no objection, please discuss matter with Prime Minister along following lines: 1)
Although Department is willing to assist Iranian Government in its endeavor to find someone with the prestige desired, it will probably be difficult and may be impossible to obtain the services of a person of such standing. At best, considerable delay may be expected. 2)
Such a man would probably have neither technical qualifications nor inclination to supervise himself a detailed reorganization and administration of Iranian financial structure. 3)
It seems likely that in any case it would be necessary to engage a practical financial expert with staff of specialized assistants, and for this work Department knows of no one so well qualified as Dr. Millspaugh. 4)
Accordingly, unless Iranian Government has some definite reason to believe that he is not suitable, Department feels best course would be to appoint Dr. Millspaugh as financial adviser, with understanding that, if desired, this Government will endeavor to find additional adviser of even higher standing to assist Iranian Government in matters of high policy. 5)
Iranian Government has requested four specialists in economics, accounting, money problems, and taxation to work under the person who may be engaged as chief financial adviser. Department believes it would be unwise to engage subordinates prior to appointment of executive, since he would doubtless desire to have a voice in their selection. In our view it would be even more undesirable to send such men out before someone has been chosen to direct and coordinate their activities.
We are bringing above considerations to attention of Iranian Minister here.
